title: Reading a lease agreement
description: Practice understanding of lease agreements, with special emphasis on consequences of breaking a lease.

assessment_items.question_content:
Dante is renting an apartment and has to pay $\$25$ every month for having a dog.

**What is this extra fee called?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Carol signed a lease with her landlord to rent an apartment from April 1, 2024 through March 31, 2025 for $\$800$ a 
month. 

**What is the name of this type of lease?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Ivan just moved into his apartment. The lease agreement has a lease start date, but no end date.

**What is the name of this type of lease?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

When you sign a lease, you are asked to pay money up front for any potential damages.

**What is this called?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

You are thinking about adopting a dog from a local shelter. Before you do, you should check to see if you are allowed to have a dog in your apartment. 

**Which section of your lease agreement will have this information? **

[[☃ radio 1]]

Susana lives alone in her apartment. Her sister and her family of four would like to move in with her temporarily.

**What part of Susana's lease agreement outlines if this is allowed?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Blair got a new job and will need to move out of his apartment in about $30$ days. He still has $6$ months left on his lease.

**What part of his lease agreement describes what he needs to do? **

[[☃ radio 1]]

A lease agreement is a contract, which means it's a legal promise between the tenant (you) and the landlord. It specifies what both of you can and can't do while you are living in the apartment.

**Which of these is a tenant's legal responsibility?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

When you live in a rental home, your landlord is responsible for all maintenance in and around the property, but is not responsible for any of your personal things lost in fire, flood, or theft. 

**What would help pay for any of the personal items?  **

[[☃ radio 1]]

Let's say you're renting a place. There's a faucet that is leaking. You've told your landlord several times, but they haven't fixed it. You're thinking about not paying your rent until they fix the leak. 

**What would most likely happen if you stopped paying rent until the leak is fixed?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: you can move out with no consequences
you'd be breaking the lease and you may get asked to leave
the landlord will fix it right away
nothing, you have a right to not pay rent if repairs are needed

assessment_items.question_content:
Imagine you're renting a house. Your lease agreement has a set of rules you agreed to follow. 

**Which of the following actions could cause you to break the lease?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: You adopt a dog, although your lease says no pets are allowed.
You are late paying rent each month.
You move out without notice and before the lease term is up.
You have a friend move in without asking the landlord.

assessment_items.question_content:
You're renting an apartment and the air conditioner stops working right when the summer heat arrives. You need your landlord to fix it quickly, but you're not sure what to do.

**What's the best action to take in this situation?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Move out of the apartment immediately.
Stop paying rent until the air conditioner is fixed.
Notify your landlord about the issue right away. If it's not fixed in a reasonable time, keep reminding and explore other legal options.
Try to fix the air conditioner on your own.
